actualExecutionPerformed: Loaded four public UCI classification datasets, ran schema_provenance_auditor and metric_stress_validator on each loaded target, trained dummy/linear/tree baselines, performed shuffled-label and seed/split stress checks, ran class-weight extension attempts, performed fresh-seed replays, and replayed two target runs in a network-off container.
disclaimer: Sovryn produces autonomous open-research artifacts, defensive publications, and open-source research evidence. It is not a patent filing system and does not provide legal patentability, legal novelty, or freedom-to-operate opinions.
hardQuestionAnswer: The toolchain was useful mostly as structured evidence packaging and anti-hype support. schema_provenance_auditor did not beat ordinary pandas checks for raw single-table discovery on these targets. metric_stress_validator added repeatable negative controls and inflation flags, but the core scores still came from normal sklearn baselines.
negativeOrPartialFindings: No official benchmark protocol was reproduced; controlled splits were used for cross-target comparability.
schema_provenance_auditor was packaging_only or dominated_by_simple_baseline on these single-table targets.
metric_stress_validator was anti_hype_support_only rather than a unique discovery tool.
At least one class-weight extension was rejected or downgraded because macro-F1 did not materially improve.
nextResearchDirection: Continue dataset and metric validation only on targets with stronger source protocols or known split risk, and require every custom tool to show value beyond a declared pandas/sklearn baseline.

optionalToolNotUsed.pytest_repro_summary: No repository/test artifact was part of Batch 11.

targetFailures.failure: The least populated classes in y have only 1 member, which is too few. The minimum number of groups for any class cannot be less than 2. Classes with too few members are: ['204']
targetFailures.label: UCI Statlog Vehicle Silhouettes

targetsSelected: UCI Optical Recognition of Handwritten Digits
title: Batch 11 Multi-Target Dataset and Metric Validation Program
whatSovrynLearned: Across four public classification targets, Sovryn learned that the custom toolchain is useful mainly when it forces structured evidence, negative controls, replay notes, and explicit downgrade decisions. For these single-table targets, ordinary pandas and sklearn baselines explained most raw findings.
